description: Package-specific validation rules for GitHub publication

search-research.name: Search Research Provider Package
search-research.description: Unified search provider with multiple backends (Tavily, Serper, Exa, Brave)

validations.description: API key documentation must be present
validations.suggestion: Add API key configuration section to README with examples for all supported providers

validations.description: All supported providers must be documented
validations.suggestion: Document each provider's API requirements, rate limits, and configuration

validations.description: Each web backend should have test coverage
validations.suggestion: Add integration tests for each search provider backend

skill-guard.name: Skill Guard Package
skill-guard.description: Constitutional compliance and validation guard for Claude Code skills

validations.suggestion: Document all constitutional compliance rules and validation patterns
Add examples showing common validation scenarios and how to resolve failures

validations.description: Hooks must use stdout, not stderr for output
validations.suggestion: Ensure all hooks use stdout or silence for output to avoid triggering error detection

loop-core.name: Loop Core Package
loop-core.description: Autonomous development loop with state management and terminal isolation

validations.suggestion: Document how terminal isolation works and why it's important for autonomous loops

validations.description: State management tests should be present
validations.suggestion: Add tests for state persistence, recovery, and isolation between loops
Add tests verifying that state doesn't leak between concurrent terminal sessions

generic.name: Generic Package Validation
generic.description: Default validation rules for all package types

validations.description: Must comply with Claude Code plugin standards
validations.suggestion: Ensure .claude-plugin/ directory exists with valid plugin.json
Ensure CI/CD workflows test on Windows, macOS, and Linux
Ensure package has proper __init__.py and can be imported without errors
Add a LICENSE file (MIT recommended for open source)
Ensure README has title, installation, usage, and license sections

validations.description: No secrets or API keys in tracked files
validations.suggestion: Ensure no secrets are committed to git. Use .gitignore and environment variables.

severity_levels.critical: Must fix before publication - blocks GitHub creation
severity_levels.error: Should fix before publication - strongly recommended
severity_levels.warning: Consider fixing before publication - quality improvement
